rationale: The citing paper examines whether using brands with performance promises changes self-efficacy and objective task performance, and frames itself as extending prior brand-effect mechanism work. The cited paper is a marketing placebo study showing product-claim effects on physiological/performance outcomes moderated by motivation, which is a close theoretical/mechanistic overlap rather than an obvious incidental citation. Given this overlap and the confirmed DOI match, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
